string 2:
Hey, thanks for coming. And here comes the person I wanted
you to meet...

Um, is this Hiroko's office? I'm, uh, Norio. I'm a high
school student, or, I should be...

Norio says he's here from the past. If that's true, I can't
really offer him any help.

Judging by the calendar... I believe I come from a time
period nearly eight years ago.

I was visiting Nishi-Shinjuku one day, and suddenly I was
caught up in a huge explosion.

When I finally came to, it was eight years later... or so it
seemed.

That sounds a lot like what happened to you.
I think we can probably trust what he's saying.
{next}Do you have any proof?
{next}Maybe it was just a dream?
{end}I need to think this through for a while.

He showed me his school handbook. It was, in fact, issued
eight years ago.

I wish... I also thought it was a dream at first, and kept
trying to wake myself up.

Okay, I'll wait here. Come on back after you've had time to
think.

So, is everything all clear now? Can we continue where we
left off?
{next}Sure.
{end}Not yet.
Excellent. Now, let's get back to Norio's story.

Well, feel free to take a rest then. Just give me a holler
when you're ready.

It seems I can't get home on my own, so I've been looking
for someone to help me.

I was able to use the cash I had on hand at an internet
cafe. That's how I found Hiroko.

It must be hard living in a time different from your own.
We've got to get you home.

I believe you're the only one capable of helping him, so see
what you can do, okay?

Is there anyone who might know of a way to get him back to
his own time?
{next}Maybe I should talk to Mirei about it...
I'll be tagging along with you, so thanks in advance!

I'll grant an exception. Luckily, I was able to connect to
his old time. There's the exit.
Wh-Wh-What is this place?! Are you sure about this?!
Hm? This doesn't look right. Please... I need this to work!
Wait! You can't go! I refuse to let you go!

Yikes! Look at the size of that thing! It's refusing to let
me go back to my own time!

I will NOT let you go home. You can't be allowed to return
to your time!

You're quite impressive. I suppose I'll leave you be for the
moment.

However, I refuse to give up. I plan to enjoy this moment a
little longer.
I had no idea that traveling through time was so dangerous.
Let's get out of here... and fast!
Is this the time period I originally came from?

Actually, now that I think about it, I do feel a sense of
calm here.

Okay, I'll try going back to my house right away! Thanks
again for all of your help!

You have accomplished your mission, so it's time for you to
return to your time as well.

Well, well, got some time on your hands today? You don't
drop by that often.

This is perfect timing. There's something I wanna talk to
you about...
Hello? Is anyone home?
I'm glad I found you. My name's Norio.

Norio? You mean the same Norio that sent me an email saying
he's from the past?
That was the me from the past. I'm the me from the present.

You helped me eight years ago. Although, I guess that was
just a short time ago for you.

Hm, I'm not sure I understand. Take it from the top and give
us all the deets.

I see... So we talked to the Norio from the past and sent
him—err, you—back to your time.

And now you're telling us that you're that version of Norio
that time traveled before.

Naturally, I knew that the two of you would encounter my
younger self today.

You both know my situation, so there's something I could use
your help with.
{next}You were supposed to go back to normal life.
{next}You want to time travel again?
{next}Maybe it was just a dream?

Yes, that was the plan. My life was going to return to
normal... or so I thought.

No, I'm through with all that. But I think my younger self
is going to show up here again.

I wish it was a dream. Actually, just saying that gives me a
strong sense of déjà vu...

You see, when I went to the future back in high school, past
me met up with future me.

What?! Isn't that dangerous?! Based on what I've seen in
movies...

...it totally screws up the future. I think it's called a
time paradox or something.

That's right. You told me the same thing eight years ago and
convinced me not to.

However, I couldn't overcome my curiosity to see what it'd
be like to meet myself...
So you went ahead and did it.

Yep. Future me was mega successful. I lived in a fancy condo
and ran an IT company.

I even had a wife, too. I married a girl named Hanae that I
liked back in high school.

It was probably a bad idea to do that, but you're still here
now, so it's okay, right?

Actually, no... Now I'm super poor and can barely even make
ends meet.

I felt my success was guaranteed, so I partied too much and
failed my entrance exam.

Still, I felt my life was destined to turn around, so I
didn't even try to do better.

It sounds like you only have yourself to blame. So, what do
you wanna talk about?

Well, the younger me is about to give in to his curiosity
and meet his future self.

When he does, he needs to see that I'm successful so that
history can right itself.

I get it. You seeing your future self succeed is the way the
timeline was meant to be.

I'm worried that if something is different, I might
disappear like in that old movie.

So I have to recreate the situation so I can avoid a time
paradox. Please help me!
Well, what should we do?
{next}Okay, we'll help you.
{next}You reap what you sow, pal.
{next}I need to think about it.
Thank you so much! I knew you'd agree to help me.

I know that as well as anybody. I'm truly sorry for my
actions, so please help me!

That's just a polite way of brushing me off, right? Please!
You're my last hope, here!

High-school me already reached out.
He said he wanted to talk.

I know I didn't get permission, but I said to meet here at
your office in about two hours.

So, in two hours, you want our help with the meeting between
your past and present selves?

That's right. At the time, I wore a nice suit. Oh, and my
wife, Hanae, was there, too.

We can easily rent a suit for you to wear, but Hanae is
gonna be a problem...

We don't have much time, either. I guess we'll just have to
call her here.

I've got her number. I asked her for it at our recent class
reunion.
Perfect. Okay, Norio. You give Hanae a call.

Meanwhile, we'll go get that suit for you. We don't have
much time, so move quickly!
So that's the situation... Would you mind giving us a hand?

My past self will be here soon, so I just need you to
pretend that we're married.

I don't know about time travel, but I've seen TV shows where
people pretend to be married.

It'll be like I'm a private investigator or something. It
sounds fun. Count me in!
Th-Thank you!

Oh, perfect timing! Here comes the high school version of
Norio now!

Hey there, future me! Wow, I'm, like, really talking to
myself, here!

Hey there, high-school me. I know you want to know all about
our life, so here goes...

Wow! I'm the CEO of an IT company AND live in a high-rise
condo?! And I marry Hanae, too?!

That's right. A famous business magazine even named me the
"Savior of the Tech Industry"!

This is unbelievable! It's like I'm guaranteed to be
successful in the future!

Um, can I say something? I don't think I can keep up this
lie any longer!
H-Hanae!

Norio! No, not you... The younger you. I'm not married to
this person. It's all a lie.
...Wait. What?

He's no CEO, either. I actually had to loan him money for
our recent class reunion.
Are you... being serious right now?

Everything has been one big lie in order to help Hiroko and
her friend.

I'm worried about the potential time paradox here, but I
must admit, I agree with Hanae.
Hiroko and {player}, right? Is she telling the truth?
{next}Yes, it's true.
{next}What do you think?
{next}Haha. We got you!
I should've known that I'd never be successful...

I know this is weird for me to say, but I trust Hanae more
than my future self.

Hey, this is serious! Let's try not to make this any more
complicated than it already is.

At any rate, I need to be honest with you, high-school
Norio.

You saw your future success and stopped trying, so that's
why you end up like this.
U-Um...
It's over, Norio. I can't be a part of this charade anymore.

Back in high school, I was attracted to your honesty, but
when did you lose that quality?

I understand. Thank you. Now, then, is it possible for me to
return to my own time?

We can send you back... right? Go ahead and send Norio here
back to his own time.
...Sorry. I guess we need a little more time!

Something's been bothering me for a while. Don't you find
this kinda strange?
{next}You mean time travel?
{next}Are you talking about Norio himself?
{next}Norio's suit?

Yeah, time travel is definitely weird, but I'm talking about
something else.
You noticed it, too? There's something off about all this.

Well, it DOES look a bit cheap for a "CEO," but I'm talking
about something else!

You and Inori were both caught up in the chaos and turned up
missing.

It was because you traveled eight years into the future.
Norio should've been the same...

So why did Norio grow up and not go missing? Does it mean
that one of them is a fake?
This woman actually has a brain on her shoulders.

I took an interest in the boy when I saw him wandering
between time and space.

I was curious what would happen if he met himself, so I
played with space-time a bit.

This is a rare opportunity. Therefore, I cannot let you go
home before I have my fun!
Stay back, Hanae! Get over here, quickly!
Agh! My legs refuse to move... Help!
Look out!
Young Norio! You saved my life... Are you all right?!
O-Ow... I'll be okay. Come this way, Hanae!

We'd just slow them down. We should probably leave this to
our friends here!
Urk... Impossible... How could this be?!

I lost... Go ahead and finish me off now. Erase me from
existence!

There's no need for that. It's not like curiosity is a bad
thing, right?

Besides, it was your mischief that allowed me the positive
experience of meeting future me.

...Well, I apologize. I'll refrain from ever engaging in
such behavior in the future.
You should go and live a happy life.

So this warping of time and space caused two versions of
Norio to come into existence.

And it goes without saying that the high school version of
Norio doesn't belong here.

Okay, this is goodbye for real, then. I'm sorry for all the
trouble.

It's all good. It was amazing having the chance to meet
you... or should I say myself?
Okay, {player}. Please send me back to my own time.
What can I do for you? Oh, it's you again.
Again? What do you mean?

I just sent you back... Wait. This is a slightly different
time from last time, huh?

Okay, I'll send the kid home, but this is the last time I'll
let a normal person through.

Is this the time period I originally came from? I do feel a
sense of calm here.

Am I really going to be such a loser in the future? Is my
fate predetermined? No...

Anyway, I'll try going back to my house now. Thanks again
for all of your help!

You have accomplished your mission, so it's time for you to
return to your time as well.

Ah, you're back. It looks like young Norio made it home
safely?

Hey there, {player}. I'm sorry for any trouble my past and
present selves caused.
Okay, we should be heading home, too. Are you ready, Hanae?
Yes.
Hm? It's been a while since you two last met, right?
Huh? No. What are you talking about? We're married.

We're going home. We live in that high-rise on the other
side of the park.
You two should come visit us sometime!
It turns out you ended up becoming successful after all?

I wrote about my experience, and it became a bestselling
book, anime, movie and musical.

When I saved future Hanae that time, I felt I could change
things if I tried hard enough.

When he came back from the future, he told me his feelings
for me. He seemed so different.

Yeah, quite a difference from the Norio who couldn't even
pay at his own reunion...

Everything's great now. I have a home and a job, as well as
a beautiful wife.

Of course, this is all thanks to the two of you sending me
back to my own time.

We should get going, Norio. You'll be late for your magazine
interview.

Oh, that's right. Well, I hope to see both of you again
someday!

I guess the younger Norio was worried about his future and
actually got serious.
Or maybe this was Norio's plan all along...
{next}He must've inspired his younger self.
{next}What do you mean?
{next}Was it all a dream?
Yeah, that must be what happened.

In other words, I think he might've used us to get what he
wanted.

I kinda wish it was a dream. It's all so confusing I don't
know what to think.

Maybe he knew that by showing his past self what he'd
become, he'd end up successful.

So perhaps there was no historical anomaly, and history
played out exactly as intended.

Oh, that reminds me. Norio gave us something to thank us.
Here you go! Nice work!
